title: connection_manager backoff not reset on relay-initiated disconnect then reconnect success
description: In connection_manager, delay_secs is reset to 1 only inside the Ok((...)) branch when consecutive_failures is also cleared. If the relay connects successfully (resets backoff), then relay_disconnect=true fires (relay hangs up), the loop falls through to the backoff section. delay_secs is still 1 from the last successful connect (correctly). BUT: after that re-attempt fails, consecutive_failures is now 1, yet delay_secs started at 1 and doubles correctly. This is actually fine on the fail path. Real issue: after a successful reconnect, delay_secs resets to 1, but consecutive_failures is set to 0 on the success path already (line 215). So no bug in normal operation. However, if relay_disconnect=true and the subsequent re-connect fails 10 times, it gives up permanently — even if the original disconnect was transient. MAX_RETRIES=10 consecutive failures to re-open a new connection after any single drop will permanently kill the retry loop with no way to restart. A user whose relay goes down for 10 minutes gets a permanent Disconnected with no recovery short of restarting the app.
